//: @see tests/bench/README.md. One entry per (language x corelib) row. `archs` lists the
footprint targets that row can actually build for; `[]` means the row has no footprint
metric (maxspeed profiles are Ir/op only). Keep sorted by id — results.txt is sorted too.
//reps: `subtract` rows only: the two rep counts whose totals are subtracted. These are
a TRADE-OFF and are per-row on purpose -- do not copy one row's value to another.
Bigger delta = less noise. The delta carries the signal and fixed per-run noise
is divided by it. At a delta of 1000 ops java decode came out 2-5% off affine
(rejected by the gate, and irreproducible run to run); at 100000 it is 0.03%.
Bigger delta = quadratically more wall clock. Every rep runs under valgrind, so
cost is reps x Ir/op. Java is ~17k Ir/op and 110000 reps is ~1 min; python is
~840k Ir/op, where the same reps would be ~20 min PER POINT (and there are 3).
So scale reps INVERSELY with the row's Ir/op: just large enough to pass the
affineness gate. A JIT row needs a big delta (it must out-weigh tier noise); a
deterministic interpreter like CPython passes at 0.0004% with a tiny one.
If a row fails the gate, raise its reps before touching anything else.
//cxx_flags: Same as `flags` minus -ffreestanding. The C++ footprint profile CANNOT
build freestanding today: the generated .hpp includes <string>/<vector>, and
libstdc++ rejects those with #error "This header is not available in freestanding
mode." That is the gap docs/plans/cpp-embedded-footprint.md exists to close
(FixedString<N>/FixedBytes sized from schema maxlen). Until it lands, the cpp rows
are measured hosted-headers-on-bare-metal-arch, so treat them as a bloat tracker
rather than a flash budget. When it lands, drop cxx_flags and expect a step down.

RV32IMC.note: no bare-metal C++ stdlib is packaged, so C only

rows.config:
generic: { emit: project }
targets: { rust: { corelib: rs-no-std } }

generic: { emit: project }
targets: { rust: { corelib: rs } }

rows.//: CPython has no JIT, so a small delta suffices: validated at 0.0004% affine.

rows.//ts: ts measures the V8 BASELINE tier (--max-opt=1 in lang/typescript.sh), not
rows.//ts1: fully-optimized TurboFan. Default V8 tiering makes Ir non-monotonic in reps and
rows.//ts2: the affineness gate rejects it; capping at the baseline JIT removes the tier
rows.//ts3: transition and makes the subtraction affine to 0.03% at warmup 2000, reps
rows.//ts4: 500/3000. A stable relative reference, not the absolute-fastest production
rows.//ts5: number. See lang/typescript.sh for the full flag rationale.
rows.config:
generic: { emit: project }
targets: { typescript: { int64: bigint } }

generic: { emit: project }
targets: { typescript: { int64: long } }
